title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
summary: WorkOS built Studio, an internal workspace that lets non-technical users ask business questions in natural language against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion. The tool generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, making subsequent executions deterministic and cost-efficient.
writeup: Garrett Galow of WorkOS presents Studio, an internal workspace that lets non-technical users ask business questions in natural language against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion. Rather than running ad hoc queries each time, Studio generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, so repeat executions are deterministic and cost-efficient. The talk covers the techniques behind this approach, including query validation, schema injection, and parallel execution. The takeaway: turning natural language requests into persistent, source-connected widgets addresses the recurring problem of business data being inaccessible to those without query skills.
